title: Plot changes
wikitext:
[]: Plot changes             Quote Post by Ark » Sun Aug 03, 2008 12:08 pm Wow, I'm flushing out like one a day here. This is a question that's bothered me for a while. I've heard people say on other forums say that Anno basically made the plot of Eva up as they went from episode to episode. Do we actually have any concrete examples of how this manifested itself? It always seemed strange to me becuase so many things seem to link up in different parts of the series. Even Kaworu is in the opening sequence as well as many other important elements of the plot.

[]: Re: Plot changes             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 03, 2008 12:24 pm Ark wrote: It always seemed strange to me becuase so many things seem to link up in different parts of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Aug 03, 2008 12:25 pm Anno didn't exactly "make up" the plot as they went along, but stuff did get changed around quite a bit. Kaworu was planned in the series from the start, I think, so its not really a surprise to see him in the OP. You might want to read the Proposal for the series, there's a lot that really changed from the planning stages to what the end product turned out to be. EDIT: The Proposal:[url]http://evageeks.org/FGP/Proposal[/url]

[]: Re: Plot changes             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Aug 03, 2008 12:32 pm Ark wrote: Wow, I'm flushing out like one a day here.

[]: Re: Plot changes             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Aug 03, 2008 3:04 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: In this way, I've always though of NGE is a like a perfectly improvised jazz piece. It builds on a standard and then plays off it from there, and its developments may occasionally recall what it's done before as a way of keeping the whole concatenated.

[]: Re: Plot changes             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 03, 2008 3:19 pm Monk Ed wrote: That is an awesome comparison I'm going to remember and pull on someone some day when they ask me what makes NGE so great. Sig'd.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Aug 03, 2008 7:06 pm things weren't made up "episode to episode", they did have a BASIC outline of the series: "Evangelion Proposal" etc. still, Anno basically openly said "we weren't sure how exactly it would end when we started working on the beginning" and it kind of grew over time ****Further, the plot and narrative devices were obviously shaped by the series going over-budget and running out of time to finish in the second half: over-budget and out of time, episode 16 onwards take on a different tone and we "see how the production was falling apart, because we wanted to share that creative process" but that doesn't exactly affect basic plot things like Eva-03, Zeruel, etc. So there are a few things that changed, and a few things that existed in prototype form I'm not sure how the Tokyo Subway sarin gas attacks affected the plot, but they're said to have affected things because the original plot was a little similar to that cult, or something...

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Sun Aug 03, 2008 7:52 pm Anno basically openly said "we weren't sure how exactly it would end when we started working on the beginning" and it kind of grew over time

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Aug 03, 2008 8:04 pm Well I know if I had an idea for a show or a story I would start from the begining. I probabley wouldn't think up an ending until about halfway though it or later.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 03, 2008 10:49 pm Writers can think and pre-plan for ages and not come up with an ending as provocative as what Anno did. Forced, planned, or not, it made people pay attention and react with passion. And surely that must be worth something.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Mon Aug 04, 2008 3:17 am I sduppose it's kinda like the Stphen King method of writing - you know how the journey's going to start, you know what the roads like, but what actually happens on that journey sort-of makes up itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Ark » Mon Aug 04, 2008 5:23 pm Thanks for the responses. Do we have any idea at what point the concept of human instrumentality came about. It's mentioned in ep2 but did they have an idea at that point or just a name?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Aug 04, 2008 5:28 pm Ark wrote: Thanks for the responses. Do we have any idea at what point the concept of human instrumentality came about. It's mentioned in ep2 but did they have an idea at that point or just a name?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 04, 2008 5:32 pm The "Human Completion Program" was there in the earliest plot summary . I confess that on first viewing the series I expected the Singularitarian-sounding "Human Enhancement Project" to be a mere throwaway remark, never revisited after that mention in ep 2.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 05, 2008 3:12 am V wrote: episode 16 onwards take on a different tone and we "see how the production was falling apart, because we wanted to share that creative process"

[]:             Quote Post by Ark » Tue Aug 05, 2008 1:08 pm So Anno's principle criticism of the Aum Shinrikyo was that they were too exclusive? That's kind of scary. So what was SEELE's vision of instrumentality before the sarin attacks?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 05, 2008 1:20 pm Ark wrote: So Anno's principle criticism of the Aum Shinrikyo was that they were too exclusive? That's kind of scary.

[]:             Quote Post by Ark » Sun Aug 10, 2008 5:00 am So considering all of that is the claim that they changed things based on ratings false?
